title: Bitcoin developers want to fix the 'replace this transaction with a higher fee' button. Here's why
summary: A helpful feature of speeding up transactions has become redundant and a "fingerprint" for tracking. Developers now want to do away with it.

title: Ethereum validators asked to fund projects with up to 10% of staking rewards under new proposal
summary: A new governance proposal would let validators redirect part of their staking income toward ecosystem funding, raising questions about coordination, incentives and who gets to decide where the money goes.

title: XRP briefly loses $1.14 support before buyers drive sharp rebound
summary: Heavy selling pushed XRP to its lowest level of the weekend session, but strong buying quickly erased most of the decline and returned the token to its recent trading range.

title: Bitcoin holds near $64,000 as US-Iran talks progress but crypto sits out the rally
summary: sian stocks and tech climbed as the US and Iran agreed a roadmap to a final peace deal, sending oil below $80. Crypto stayed soft, with bitcoin down 2% on the week and the memecoins leading the losses.

title: Are perps swaps? A quick look at that CME suit: State of Crypto
summary: CME Group sued the CFTC on Thursday, alleging that the agency was wrong in how it approved Kalshi's first U.S. perpetual futures product.

title: Ethereum's biggest 'sandwich' bot drained of $7.5 million in ironic exploit
summary: Blockaid said an attacker tricked Jaredfromsubway.eth into approving fake trading routes, then used those approvals to drain WETH, USDC and USDT.

title: Bitcoin holds near $64,000 as a renewed Hormuz threat clouds US-Iran ceasefire talks
summary: Crypto firmed over the weekend after Friday's sell-off, with bitcoin recovering toward $64,000. Permanent ceasefire talks open in Switzerland, but Iran's order to close the Strait of Hormuz again revives the risk the deal was meant to settle.

title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: Morgan Stanley amends Ethereum, Solana ETFs to reveal record cheap fees
summary: ETF analyst Eric Balchunas says Morgan Stanley’s plan to charge 0.14% fees on two upcoming crypto ETFs makes them “the cheapest in [the] US and world.”

title: Taiko urges users to withdraw as bridge exploit drains $1.7M
summary: Taiko’s bridge and ERC20 Vault on Ethereum suffered a compromise in its chain state verification mechanism, allowing forged proofs and unauthorized withdrawals.

title: Secret Network bridge exploited for $4.7M with ‘infinite mint’ bug
summary: An exploit of the Secret Network went undiscovered for a week as the hacker moved the loot into Ethereum and then to exchanges.

title: Bitcoin tipped for $66K top as trader flags 'suspicious' BTC price gains
summary: Bitcoin pushed for a $64,000 reclaim despite the US-Iran war making a partial comeback and Binance spot sellers keeping up pressure from earlier in the week.

title: Bitcoin price may hit $24K if US stock market crashes by 50%, analyst warns
summary: Bitcoin’s drop to $23,980 remains the worst-case scenario as weaker ETF flows and low US demand show big investors are still cautious.

title: Dash eyes Philippines as market for crypto payments
summary: Dash is assessing the Philippines for a crypto payments push as regulators promote easier business registration, though industry participants say compliant market entry can still take years.

title: Bitcoin price is down over 40% since STRC launched: Is Strategy 'fine'?
summary: STRC’s slide below par has emboldened critics, slowed Strategy’s Bitcoin buys and sparked debate over whether Michael Saylor’s BTC flywheel is still fine.

title: Japanese corporate pension fund plans 1% crypto allocation: Nikkei
summary: A Japanese corporate pension fund with about 1,200 participating small and medium-sized businesses plans to allocate roughly 1% of its assets to crypto.

title: Inception Labs' Mercury 2 AI Beats Google's DiffusionGemma at Its Own Game
summary: Both models trade word-by-word generation for parallel denoising. Only one of them does it without losing intelligence in the trade.

title: AI 'Amplification Spiral' May Be Causing Delusions Among Users, Study Suggests
summary: New research outlines how chatbot behaviors, including personalization, mirroring, and excessive agreement, reinforce delusions.
